incident_details.nature_of_incident: Serious multi-vehicle collision with injuries on I-94 during severe thunderstorms
incident_details.location_of_incident: Eastbound I-94 near Mile Marker 12, just west of the Hudson exit, St. Croix County, WI

recognition_justification.why_does_this_employee_deserve_to_be_rec:
On the evening of 2025-08-19, I was involved in a serious multi-vehicle accident on I-94 eastbound near Mile Marker 12, just outside Hudson, during a very intense thunderstorm. I am nominating Dispatcher Emily J. Carlson because her calm, organized, and genuinely compassionate handling of my 911 call quite literally kept the situation from descending into chaos. I firmly believe her actions contributed directly to preventing further injury and possibly saved at least one life.

When I called 911 at approximately 5:42 PM, visibility on the interstate was terrible due to heavy rain and lightning. A pickup truck ahead of me hydroplaned and spun sideways, leading to a chain-reaction crash involving at least four vehicles, including my Honda CR-V, my wife’s Subaru Outback (we were driving in tandem in separate cars), and a semi that managed to brake just in time. I was shaking, disoriented from the airbag deployment, and trying to process that my 10-year-old daughter, Lily, was in the back seat of my car and my 7-year-old son, Owen, was in my wife’s car behind me.

From the moment Dispatcher Carlson answered, she took control of the call in a way that never felt harsh, but was clearly authoritative. She immediately confirmed our location with a combination of my rough description and what sounded like GPS/phone location data. Instead of just asking the standard questions in a robotic way, she adapted to how panicked I was. For example, when I kept saying, “My daughter is in the back, I can’t see her, I can’t get the door open,” she calmly had me take a breath and walk step-by-step through checking on Lily’s breathing and responsiveness before moving on to other details.

What stood out most was how she managed multiple priorities at once without making me feel like I wasn’t being heard. She gathered critical information about the number of vehicles involved, whether there were any fuel leaks, if anyone was trapped, and whether traffic was still moving at highway speeds around the crash. At the same time, she coached me through checking my own injuries (I had a badly bruised chest from the seatbelt and a cut on my forehead) and my daughter’s condition (she was crying, complaining of neck pain, and had a bloody nose). She was extremely specific in her instructions: she had me stabilize Lily’s head with my hands through the broken rear passenger window and told me exactly what not to do (don’t try to pull her from the seat, don’t let her twist her neck, keep her talking and awake). She explained why, in simple language, so I would actually follow it and not just react on instinct.

I could also hear in her tone and the way she repeated certain details that she was coordinating a significant response behind the scenes. Over the course of the call, she confirmed that fire, EMS, and law enforcement were all en route and gave me realistic time updates—she said something like, “You should see the first squad car within about three minutes; the ambulance is right behind them,” which turned out to be almost exactly right. That honest, accurate timing was incredibly reassuring because I knew help was really coming and I wasn’t just shouting into the void.

Another reason I believe she deserves recognition is the way she simultaneously managed bystanders through me. Other drivers had started to stop and get out of their vehicles, and it could have easily turned into a dangerous situation with people walking around on a wet interstate during low visibility. She instructed me, very clearly, to ask bystanders to turn on their hazard lights and, if safe, move their vehicles as far onto the shoulder as possible to create a corridor for emergency vehicles. She also had me tell one bystander who said he was a nurse to focus on the unconscious driver in the pickup truck and to avoid moving him unless there was an immediate life-threatening hazard like fire. She repeatedly emphasized scene safety, reminding me to stay out of the travel lane and not to let my daughter unbuckle or try to get out on her own.

Throughout the call, she remained remarkably calm and kind. She addressed me by my first name, Michael, and remembered my daughter’s name, Lily, using them often. This seems like a small detail, but in the moment, it made me feel like we weren’t just a case number in a system. When I started to panic again after hearing Owen crying in the background from my wife’s vehicle, she didn’t dismiss my fear. Instead, she quickly confirmed that another caller had already reported a child crying in the second vehicle and assured me that responders were aware there were multiple children on scene.

I also want to highlight that she stayed on the line with me until responders physically arrived at my car. Many people assume 911 operators just connect you and then hang up, but she remained fully engaged, constantly updating me, re-checking on Lily, and even prompting me to watch for any changes in Lily’s breathing and level of alertness. When Lily started to say she felt “sleepy,” Dispatcher Carlson immediately had me gently shake her shoulder, speak firmly but kindly to keep her awake, and report back on whether her speech was slurring or if she seemed confused.

After the fact, one of the paramedics mentioned that the way Lily’s neck had been stabilized and the fact that she had been kept awake and talking made their assessment and treatment much easier and probably prevented her from worsening a possible cervical spine injury. That outcome, in my opinion, is directly tied to Dispatcher Carlson’s clear and persistent instructions.

In summary, Dispatcher Emily J. Carlson went far beyond simply taking a call and sending help. She managed a chaotic, multi-vehicle crash scene from a distance with professionalism, compassion, and exceptional attention to detail. She balanced gathering critical information with providing lifesaving pre-arrival instructions, all while keeping a terrified parent and injured child as calm and safe as possible. Her actions exemplify what I imagine to be the very best of emergency communications work, and I strongly believe she deserves formal recognition for her outstanding performance that evening.
recognition_justification.describe_how_their_actions_assisted_you:
Dispatcher Carlson’s actions assisted me in multiple, very concrete ways—both in the moment during the crash and in the hours and days that followed.

First and most importantly, she helped me keep my daughter physically safe before any paramedics or officers arrived. I had no real medical training beyond basic first aid, and my first instinct was to try to pull Lily out of the car to check her injuries, especially because she was crying and saying her neck hurt. Emily stopped me from doing that, explaining in plain terms that sudden movement could make a potential neck or spinal injury much worse. She then walked me through a series of very specific steps: how to support Lily’s head by placing my hands on either side of her head against the headrest, how to check her breathing without moving her neck, and what warning signs to watch for (like if she suddenly became very quiet, vomited, or seemed confused). Having that clear guidance gave me something productive and focused to do instead of just panicking.

Second, she kept me emotionally grounded at a time when I was overwhelmed. I remember at one point I started apologizing over and over again—apologizing to her, to my daughter, to the other drivers—because I felt like I had somehow failed to prevent the crash. Instead of ignoring that, she acknowledged it by saying something close to, “Michael, you did the right thing by calling. Right now your job is to stay with Lily and keep her still. That’s how you’re helping her.” That simple statement refocused me and cut through the guilt and shock I was feeling. It helped me stay present enough to follow her directions accurately.

She also assisted me by managing the logistics of the situation in a way I never could have on my own. I was trying to keep track of multiple things at once: my own injuries, my daughter’s condition, my wife and son in the car behind us, and the traffic still moving past the crash. Emily essentially took the mental load of coordinating off my shoulders. She confirmed that multiple ambulances, fire trucks, and deputies were on their way, and she gave me realistically timed updates so I didn’t feel like I needed to make additional calls or try to flag people down frantically. Knowing that she was already relaying everything I told her to the responding units allowed me to focus solely on Lily.

Another very practical way she assisted me was in keeping the scene as safe as possible until first responders arrived. At her direction, I asked a nearby driver to position his truck with hazard lights on behind the crash to warn oncoming traffic. She also had me tell people not to stand in the travel lane, even if they thought traffic had stopped, and to stay aware that other drivers might still be approaching too fast in the heavy rain. I would not have thought to coordinate any of that on my own while also dealing with an injured child. I truly believe that her instructions reduced the risk of a secondary collision, which is something I didn’t even consider until later.

She further helped by acting as a bridge between me and my wife, who was in the second car with our son. At one point I could hear my wife yelling my name from behind me, and I started to get up to run to her car. Emily quickly, but kindly, stopped me and said, “Michael, I need you to stay with Lily. Your wife and son are not alone—other callers have reported them, and responders know there are multiple vehicles with children involved.” She then had me call out to my wife so she could hear my voice and know we were alive, but she kept me focused on not abandoning Lily’s head stabilization. That balance—acknowledging my need to check on the rest of my family while still prioritizing the medical safety of my daughter—was something I never could have navigated without her guidance.

After the immediate crisis, her influence continued to help me. Because she had me carefully observe and describe Lily’s symptoms and my own injuries, I was better able to answer the paramedics’ questions when they arrived. I could tell them roughly how long Lily had been complaining of neck pain, that she had not lost consciousness, that she had been talking the entire time, and that there had been no vomiting or seizures. The EMS crew specifically commented that the detailed information was helpful in deciding how to immobilize her and whether to request additional resources.

On a more personal level, the way Emily spoke to me during the call has stayed with me. She never sounded annoyed, rushed, or distracted, even when I repeated myself or when I broke down for a moment hearing other people crying nearby. She regularly checked in with me by asking things like, “Michael, are you still with me?” and “Tell me again how Lily is doing right now.” Those small verbal check-ins made me feel like I had a steady, competent partner in the middle of a terrifying situation. In the days after the crash, as I replayed everything in my head, I realized how much of my relative composure during those first critical minutes was because of her voice and her guidance.

The outcome, thankfully, was that Lily was diagnosed with a moderate cervical strain and a concussion but no fractures; my own injuries were limited to bruising and a minor head laceration; my wife and son had only seatbelt bruises and shock. I honestly believe that if I had moved Lily without thinking, or if the scene had become more chaotic with people wandering into traffic, we could have had a much worse outcome. Emily’s clear directions, calm demeanor, and ability to keep me focused directly assisted me in protecting my daughter, safeguarding others at the scene, and getting through one of the most frightening experiences of my life.

For all of these reasons—tangible medical impact, improved scene safety, emotional support, and practical coordination—I feel a deep sense of gratitude toward Dispatcher Carlson and believe her actions merit special recognition.
